Definition: Entire lifecycle of waste including generation, collection, transport, recovery and disposal of waste.
Proactive and systematic monitoring, control and optimization of energy consumption.
Monitoring, reporting and reduction of GHG emissions and air pollutants affecting human health and the environment.
Effective oversight of water withdrawal, consumption and discharge to minimise environmental impact.
Systems and processes to address ESG and operational challenges across the supply chain.
Organisation efforts to interact and build relationships with communities to create positive social impact.
Fostering a workforce reflecting varied backgrounds and creating an inclusive environment with equal opportunities.
Fundamental rights for all individuals, eliminating child and forced labour and harassment throughout operations and supply chains.
Practices to safeguard data from unauthorised access and compliance with data privacy laws.
Implementing a safe and healthy work environment, preventing workplace hazards and promoting employee well-being.
Ensuring products meet defined standards of reliability and safety, preventing defects that could pose risks to users.
Commitment to conducting business with integrity and openness, focusing on ethical behaviour and anti-competitive conduct.
Adherence to national and international legal, regulatory and industry-specific standards governing operations.
Designing products that minimise environmental and social impacts across their lifecycle from raw material to disposal.
Entity economic value creation and distribution, reflecting overall economic impact on society.
Strategy addressing how operations affect natural habitats, species and ecosystems particularly in sensitive areas.
Practices for managing customer interactions to enhance satisfaction, loyalty and trust through transparent communication.
Systematic process of investing in and developing new products, processes or technologies to improve efficiency.
Strategies to retain skilled employees, reduce turnover and provide professional development opportunities.
Processes for selecting and overseeing suppliers to meet operational needs and ESG criteria.
All employees in upstream and downstream value chain who can be materially impacted by the organisation.
